topology.summary: Two declared DUs each own one named cell; one UE path is reviewed against the declared DU pair.

blast_radius.max_change_scope: two named DUs and their declared cells
blast_radius.operator_impact: A bad change may disrupt the declared DU pair and their transport assumptions, but it must not imply generalized distributed-DU support.
blocked_until: DU ownership and cutover order are reviewable
rollback target is named for both DUs
verify can report DU-local state transitions

approval.approval_scope: declared two-DU cutover rehearsal or future proof run
required_checks: the DU pair and transport assumptions match the profile
the single-UE fallback path remains visible
reviewers can inspect DU-local failure domains

restore_actions: restore the prior DU assignment for the declared UE path
revert any transport-path changes made for the second DU
rollback_triggers: the declared DU pair can no longer explain their verify outcome

success_criteria: each declared DU has explicit gate-class and rollback state
the UE path can be explained without hidden transport assumptions

manual_review: confirm the topology note still describes this as profile-defined and not runtime-proven

non_claims: no multi-cell parity claim beyond the declared DU pair
no broad topology parity claim outside the declared profile
